headline: Sarawak Proposes State Immigration Appeals Tribunal
context: Sarawak proposed establishing state immigration appeals tribunal in January 2026 to adjudicate entry denial cases independently from federal courts.

cards.big: Sarawak proposes state immigration tribunal independent of federal courts
cards.sub: Move gives Sarawak exclusive jurisdiction over immigration appeal processes

cards.big: Federal courts overturned 28 Sarawak immigration decisions in 2025
cards.sub: State says federal judicial intervention undermines MA63 autonomy rights

cards.big: Proposed tribunal would have 5 judges appointed by Sarawak Governor
cards.sub: Attorney General Chambers warned this could create parallel legal systems

cards.big: 12 ASEAN diplomats protested Sarawak entry denials in 2025
cards.sub: Foreign Ministry mediated 8 cases where state decisions conflicted federal visas

cards.big: Parallel tribunals test whether state autonomy can extend to judicial institutions
A state immigration appeals tribunal tests whether Sarawak can build independent judicial institutions.
